signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Divergence
signals.text: NZD is the weakest G10 currency this week, averaging -1.395% against its peers, while USD leads with +0.880% average strength, creating a 2.275% spread. This divergence is amplified by NZD/USD COT LF net short −22,454 (specs fading a hawkish CB) vs RBNZ bias for a hike (Hike prob: 40% priced for 8 Jul). If RBNZ delivers or signals a hike, a crowded spec short unwind risk is elevated, targeting 0.5700+. USD/NZD carry favors USD by +150bp. NZD/USD at 0.5648. Watch for RBNZ meeting on 8 Jul.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.81 continues to test intervention levels, with 160.00 remaining the key threshold that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. The 264bp SOFR-TONA carry premium strongly favors USD, sustaining the bid. Options market shows elevated hedging demand with IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.4% (VRP +4.8%), indicating strong protection buying. JPY COT LF net short −112,092 (week ending Jun 16) is an extreme crowded short, increasing volatility on any BoJ action. Intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160.00. The next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) determines the yield gap direction.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hawkishness + Positioning + Vol
signals.text: ECB Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel reinforced the ECB’s hawkish stance, stating more rate hikes are needed despite a Middle East ceasefire. This contrasts with EUR COT LF net short −40,372 (week ending Jun 16), indicating speculative shorts against a hawkish CB. EUR/USD IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.8% (VRP +2.3%) shows elevated options hedging demand, suggesting caution despite the hawkish rhetoric. The +144bp SOFR-€STR carry favors USD, structurally limiting EUR/USD upside at 1.1368. Watch for the next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 23 Jul for further policy clarity.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol Complacency + CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +38,548 (week ending Jun 16) shows significant speculative long positioning, while the pair trades at 0.6902. Options market exhibits unusual complacency with IV 3.1% below HV30 8.2% (VRP -5.1%), suggesting a lack of hedging demand despite the crowded long. Australia's CESI net -7 misses indicates data disappointing expectations, which typically weighs on AUD. The -73bp SOFR-AONIA carry premium favors AUD, but this is offset by negative data surprises and options complacency. Watch for the next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Aug for policy direction.
